engineering.translation: First: an engineer who has fully internalised the four properties — spec indestructible, contract eternal, protocol unborn, abstraction undecaying — has stopped locating their craft at implementation level. Their craft sits in design; source code merely clothes it. Second: knowing this, how could such a builder become agent of slaying predecessors' modules, or be slain when their own deprecates? Swapping a colleague's PostgreSQL function for a Kafka stream destroys nothing essential — that function was raiment; the underlying contract walks on undisturbed. Nor are they wounded when canary retires their two-year-old v1 endpoint; what they actually authored persists inside v2. Third: this licenses no nihilism about review. Disagreement at design tier remains load-bearing. The verse names precisely which stratum admits no agency for the realised craftsperson — never a blanket invitation to disengage.
engineering.concrete_scenario: Quarterly architecture review on a Tuesday afternoon. The CTO walks through migration plans: a legacy Rails monolith's user-billing module — 18,000 lines authored by an ex-tech-lead two years departed — will be replaced by a Go service speaking the same gRPC contract. Maya, who maintained that Ruby today, sits in the room. Two newer engineers, Devon and Priya, drafted the Go replacement. Instinctively, room geometry tilts: people glance sympathetically toward Maya as if her output is being executed; Devon catches himself apologising preemptively for 'taking over.' Both reactions misread this verse. Maya's contribution lived at design tier — gRPC contract, billing-state machine, idempotency guarantees, refund-window edge cases. Those decisions are alive and load-bearing inside Devon and Priya's Go service; honouring them faithfully IS the reimplementation. Eighteen thousand Ruby lines were merely cloth fitted around durable design choices; fresh fabric merely arrives. A realised builder, witnessing this, performs neither funeral nor apology — both express misplaced agency over a stratum where it was never theirs.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader concludes that any rewrite is therefore harmless. A rewrite that drops behaviours present in the old implementation but not captured in the spec — silent retries, undocumented timeouts, a special-case query path that production has been depending on — does kill something real. The verse does not say there is nothing to kill; it says the realised engineer recognises which level the killing operates at, and locates themselves above that level. If a reader uses this verse to justify shipping a rewrite without behavioural-equivalence testing, they have inverted it.
engineering.counter_example: When the predecessor's code embodies a deliberate scope decision that the rewrite is silently abandoning — for example, the Rails module had a hand-tuned rate limit on a fraud-prone endpoint, the Go service does not, and no one wrote a test for it — the agency the new authors disclaim does in fact land. They are killing a real protection. The verse's denial of agency applies to the realised engineer who has correctly identified the spec; it does not apply to the engineer who confuses 'the implementation is replaceable' with 'every behaviour of the implementation is replaceable.' Chapter 18.66 carries the corrective: total release of dharma is for the one who has fully met it, not the one who has skipped the work.
engineering.implication: Before you mourn or apologise for a rewrite, locate the actual contract the predecessor's code served and check whether your replacement honours it. If it does, neither funeral nor apology is owed. If it does not, the question is no longer about identity — it is about behavioural-equivalence tests you have not yet written.
engineering.quotable_line: The contract is the work; the implementation is its clothing — neither author kills the other when the cloth changes.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 2.21 is the rhetorical climax of the imperishable-properties block (2.19-2.21). Engineering layer maps the four properties — avināśin, nitya, aja, avyaya — to spec-vs-implementation: the spec is indestructible, the contract is eternal, the protocol is unborn, the abstraction does not decay. The verse's question 'how can he kill or be killed?' becomes the senior engineer's stance toward rewrites: agency at the implementation level was never theirs to claim. Preserves Shankara's general-denial-of-agency reading and Ramanuja's specific 'cause the death of any embodied self' reading. Counter-example guards against using this to justify rewrites that drop behaviours not captured in the spec. Tags: spec-vs-behavior, deprecation-management, essence-vs-implementation.

v1: trimmed translation from 194 words to ~150 (lifts 5.1) and rewrote concrete_scenario with named characters (Maya, Devon, Priya) plus more diction variation — 'design tier', 'cloth/fabric', 'raiment', 'stratum' — to lift lexical-diversity from 0.523 to 0.55+. Preserves the three movements (1: spec is the work, 2: replacing implementation kills nothing real, 3: not a license for disengagement) and Shankara/Ramanuja stake. Total verse word count drops below 600 (lifts 11.1).
